critical.name: Missing HTTPS/TLS Configuration
critical.description: Spring Boot application is not enforcing HTTPS. All traffic should be encrypted.
critical.evidence: Server allows HTTP connections without redirect to HTTPS
critical.remediation: Enable Spring Security SSL configuration and HTTP to HTTPS redirect

critical.name: Missing Authentication on Admin Endpoints
critical.description: Admin endpoints are accessible without authentication, allowing unauthorized access to sensitive operations.
critical.evidence: Admin endpoints return 200 OK without auth headers
critical.remediation: Implement Spring Security @EnableWebSecurity with endpoint protection and JWT/OAuth2

medium.name: Missing CSRF Token Validation
medium.description: CSRF tokens are not validated on state-changing operations (POST/PUT/DELETE)
medium.evidence: No X-CSRF-Token header validation in requests
medium.remediation: Enable Spring Security CSRF protection with token generation and validation

medium.name: Insecure Deserialization in Spring Boot
medium.description: Spring Boot may deserialize untrusted data, allowing RCE attacks
medium.owasp_category: A08:2021 - Software and Data Integrity Failures
medium.evidence: Jackson ObjectMapper configured without type restrictions
medium.remediation: Enable Jackson type safety features: JsonTypeInfo annotations and typer modules

medium.name: Insufficient Logging and Monitoring
medium.description: Security events are not properly logged for audit trail and threat detection
medium.owasp_category: A09:2021 - Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
medium.evidence: No structured security event logging (auth attempts, errors, data access)
medium.remediation: Implement SLF4J with structured logging for all security events

medium.name: Missing Security Headers
medium.description: Critical security headers are missing from HTTP responses
medium.remediation: Configure Spring Security headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options

low.name: Verbose Error Messages
low.description: Error messages reveal sensitive information about the application stack
low.evidence: Stack traces visible in error responses with Groq API details
low.remediation: Implement custom error handlers that mask sensitive details in production

low.name: Default Security Headers Not Configured
low.description: Server reveals version information in headers
low.evidence: Response headers show Flask/Werkzeug version numbers
low.remediation: Configure custom Server headers and remove version information
